styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. HALL OUTSIDE BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. Midnight. A dark lantern, fully closed, in THE NARRATOR's hand. He turns the latch-oh, so gently-and eases the door open a crack. He slides the lantern in, then, inch by inch. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "a dark lantern, all closed, closed, that no light shone out, and then I thrust". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, soft slippers, quiet tread Camera directive: Close-up of dark lantern sliding inch by inch through the opening, 50mm, no light escaping the closed shutters. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Feverish wide-eyed concentration with tight unnatural half-smile Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, creamy background bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, near-black voids, cold blue-steel shadows, sparse warm amber lantern spill
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "a dark lantern, all closed, closed, that no light shone out, and then I thrust". Start speaking immediately with "a" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient/Foley: door open, lantern slide, latch turn. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. HALL OUTSIDE BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. Midnight. A dark lantern, fully closed, in THE NARRATOR's hand. He turns the latch-oh, so gently-and eases the door open a crack. He slides the lantern in, then, inch by inch. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "And then, when I had made an opening sufficient for my head, I put in". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, soft slippers, quiet tread Camera directive: Medium shot over narrator's shoulder as door cracks open, 35mm lens, slow push toward the dark gap. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Intense focused stare with jaw clenched rigid and brows lifted Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field, soft environmental falloff Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, near-black voids, cold blue-steel shadows, sparse warm amber lantern spill
